assertions.description: <script type="speculationrules"> is recognised as a non-executing data script; this is the carrier for speculation rules.
document.prerendering is exposed so pages can detect they are being prerendered as a result of a speculation rule.
document fires prerenderingchange / has the onprerenderingchange surface, the signal pages use to detect activation after speculative prerender.
PerformanceNavigationTiming.activationStart is exposed for measuring when a prerendered page was activated.
Speculation Rules accepts an 'eagerness' field with values 'eager', 'moderate', 'conservative', and 'immediate'; the values are JSON keywords the parser accepts.
No-Vary-Search support is the cache-side companion; speculation prefetches benefit from cache hits using the same Headers surface.
